message:
fix(hostcall): fail-closed zero-divisor handling in drop rate and observability overhead calculations

Both hostcall_conformance_governance and hostcall_session_governance_gate had
division-by-zero guards that returned 0 (pass) when the denominator was zero
but the numerator was non-zero. This is incorrect fail-open behavior — if
there are drops but no total replays, or overhead but no uninstrumented
throughput, the correct fail-closed result is 1_000_000 (100%).

hostcall_conformance_governance.rs:
- ReplayDropEntry::new(): when total_replays=0 and drop_count>0, return
  drop_rate_millionths=1_000_000 and within_budget=false
- ObservabilityClaimDelta::relative_drift_millionths(): when baseline=0
  and delta>0, return 1_000_000 instead of 0
- New tests: drop_entry_nonzero_drop_zero_total_fails_closed,
  claim_delta_zero_baseline (updated expectation),
  claim_delta_zero_baseline_zero_delta (new, verifies 0/0=0)

hostcall_session_governance_gate.rs:
- ReplayDropRecord::new(): same fail-closed fix for zero total_count
- ObservabilityDelta::new() and ::with_max_overhead(): same fail-closed fix
  for zero uninstrumented_throughput
- New test: replay_drop_nonzero_zero_total_fails_closed
- Updated: observability_delta_zero_uninstrumented (now asserts 1_000_000)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
